sections.heading: The purpose of Extend
sections.body:
Extend your fitness tracking and planning with this easy to use app. Log exercises, weights, sets, reps and durations. No subscriptions, no ads, no unneccesary bloat.

Personal records, volume trends, streaks, muscle distribution, and more. Important stats and charts you want to focus on, are readily available where you want them.

sections.heading: What You Can Track
sections.body: Olympic weights, kettlebells, dumbbells, cardio, bodyweight — any exercise you do. Build routines, log sessions, and watch your progress and personal records build over time.

sections.heading: Customization
sections.body: Make it yours. Rearrange the navigation bar(s), build a custom Dashboard with the stats and shortcuts you care about, choose light or dark theme. Customize what you see and how you access it.

sections.heading: Getting Started Fast
sections.body:
1. Review muscles.
2. Review equipment.
3. Review exercises.
4. Build workout routines. Standard exercise sets, complexes, supersets, circuits. Lots of options to choose from. Need a voice to speak while working out? Explore the Voice Trainer module. Just need a timer? Explore a variety in the Timer module.
5. Do a workout! Want one selected randomly for you, explore the Generate Workout module. Want to do a single exercise quickly? Tap [sf:play.circle.fill] on any exercise in the Exercises module.
6. Save what you've completed and review your progress in the Log and in all related modules.
7. Optionally, set up a Training Plan to schedule workouts and exercises across your week.

title: Settings
summary: Theme, units, navigation, dashboard, HealthKit, and data import/export.

sections.heading: Navigation Bar(s)
sections.body:
Add, remove, and reorder modules across the top and bottom navigation bars. Each bar is configured independently.

• Color — set a background color, optional gradient, gradient direction, and text color.
• Customize — opens the full nav bar editor to add, remove, and reorder module icons.

sections.heading: Dashboard
sections.body:
Configure the appearance and contents of your Dashboard.

• Color — set a background color and optional gradient for the Dashboard.
• Customize — opens the tile editor to add, remove, resize, and reorder tiles.

Tile types:
• Module Shortcut — opens a module.
• Stat Card — shows a live stat.
• Shortcut — launches a specific workout, timer, voice trainer, or exercise.
• Blank — fill in space with an animated emoticon.

Stat cards available: Total Workouts, Day Streaks, Longest Streak, Total Time, Favorite Exercise, Favorite Day, Workout Frequency (14 days), Muscle Group Distribution (7 days), Volume This Week, Rest Days (14 days), Personal Record, 1RM Leaderboard.

Each tile supports: custom title, icon, size (small/medium/large), accent color and placement (none/left/top/right/bottom), and optional background tint. Tap [sf:gear] on any tile to access its configuration.

sections.heading: System Preferences
sections.body:
• Theme — Light or Dark. (The Stick Figure Editor, Animation Studio, and Match Game always stay light regardless of this setting.)
• Image Set — chooses the built-in anatomy images used in workout and muscle views — Option 1, Option 2, or None.
• Weight Unit — lbs or kg. Affects all weight display and entry throughout the app — logs, stat cards, personal records.
• Apple Health — Export: sends completed workout sessions to Apple Health. Import Activities: pulls workout data from Health into your Log. Sync Now: manually trigger a sync.
• Keep Screen On During Sessions — prevents the screen from locking during active timers, workouts, and voice trainer sessions. Uses more battery.

sections.heading: Data
sections.body:
• Export Workouts — exports selected workout routines as a JSON file. Useful as a backup or for transferring to another device. Exports routine definitions only, not logged session history.
• Import Workouts — imports workout routines from a previously exported JSON file. Missing exercises, muscles, or equipment are created automatically.
• Export Plans — exports selected training plans as a JSON file, including all referenced workouts, exercises, muscles, equipment, voice trainer configs, and timer configs.
• Import Plans — imports training plans from a previously exported plan JSON file. All dependencies are resolved by name — created if missing, matched if found.
• Export Log Data — exports your full session history as a CSV file.
• Reset App — deletes everything: logs, routines, exercises, muscles, equipment, timers, voice trainer configs, training plans, and all customizations. Cannot be undone.

sections.heading: Help Center
sections.body: Opens this help system. Browse articles by module, or search for any topic.

sections.heading: About
sections.body:
• App Version and Build number.
• Developer Website link.
• Privacy Policy link.

sections.heading: Support the Developer
sections.body: If you enjoy Extend, consider leaving a tip via PayPal or Venmo. No pressure — just appreciated.

title: Dashboard
summary: Home screen with customizable stat tiles and shortcuts.

sections.heading: Tiles
sections.body:
The Dashboard is a grid of tiles you build yourself. Choose which ones, and in which order they appear. Each tile can be a module shortcut, a stat card, or a shortcut to a specific workout, exercise, timer, or voice trainer config.

Sizes: small (1 column), medium (2 columns), large (full row).

sections.heading: Stat Cards
sections.body:
• Total Workouts — lifetime count.
• Day Streaks — current consecutive days.
• Longest Streak — best streak ever.
• Total Time — total logged time.
• Favorite Exercise — most performed.
• Favorite Day — bar chart by day of week.
• Workout Frequency (14 days) — daily activity dots.
• Muscle Group Distribution — pie chart, last 7 days.
• Volume This Week — bar chart, last 7 weeks. Configurable per Workout or Exercise.
• Rest Days (14 days) — non-workout days in past two weeks.
• Personal Record — recent PRs. Configurable per Exercise.
• 1RM Leaderboard — top estimated one-rep maxes. Configurable per Exercise.

sections.heading: Shortcut Tiles
sections.body:
A Shortcut tile links to a specific saved workout, exercise, timer, or voice trainer config.

Workout, exercise, timer, and voice trainer shortcuts use a 3-piece tile:
• Top — tap to launch the item directly.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats for that item.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens session history for that item.

title: Workouts
summary: Build routines and log training sessions.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body:
• [sf:plus] — create a new workout routine.
• [sf:magnifyingglass] — search your workout list.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each workout row shows buttons:
• [sf:play.circle.fill] — start a session immediately.
• [sf:star] / [sf:star.fill] — toggle favorite (pins to the Favorites grid at the top).
• [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — view session history for this workout.
• [sf:chart.bar] — view stats (volume, frequency) for this workout.
• [sf:pencil] — edit the workout routine.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited workouts appear as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top — tap to launch the workout.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens history.

sections.heading: Creating a Workout
sections.body:
Tap [sf:plus]. Name it, then add exercises from your library.

Exercises can be left ungrouped — each is performed independently.

Or group them together. Each group gets a color and a type:
• Complex — exercises performed back to back with no rest, usually using the same weight/implement throughout.
• Superset — two or more exercises performed back to back, typically targeting opposing muscle groups.
• Circuit — three or more exercises cycled through in sequence, usually for conditioning or time efficiency.

Tap the group label to modify the configuration.

sections.heading: Logging a Session
sections.body: Tap [sf:play.circle.fill] to start. Log each exercise by entering sets (weight, reps, duration). Skip forward or back. Previous session values are shown as reference. A history sheet is a tap away. 'Complete Workout' when done and the session will appear in the log.

sections.heading: ⓘ Button (during session)
sections.body: Tap the ⓘ button next to an exercise during a session to expand its detail panel. This shows a larger muscle anatomy diagram for that exercise (if muscles are assigned) and any reference image you've added for how to perform it.

sections.heading: Personal Records, 1RM and Volume
sections.body: The app tracks PRs, estimated one-rep maxes, and Volume automatically as you log sets. View them on the Dashboard (stat cards) or per-exercise in the Exercises module.

title: Exercises
summary: Your exercise library — create, organize, launch quick sessions, and track stats per movement.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body:
• [sf:plus] — add a new exercise.
• [sf:magnifyingglass] — search the exercise list.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each exercise row shows buttons:
• [sf:play.circle.fill] — launch an immediate single-exercise session (no routine needed).
• [sf:star] / [sf:star.fill] — toggle favorite (pins to the Favorites grid).
• [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — view session history for this exercise.
• [sf:chart.bar] — view stats (total sets, average weight, PRs, 1RM).
• [sf:pencil] — edit the exercise.

sections.heading: Tapping a Row
sections.body: Tapping anywhere on an exercise row also launches a quick session for that exercise.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited exercises appear as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top [sf:flame.fill] — tap to launch a quick session.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.trianglehead.counterclockwise.rotate.90] — opens history.

sections.heading: Quick Sessions
sections.body: Launching an exercise opens a full workout session for just that one movement. Log sets, reps, and weight, then complete it. The session is saved to the Log.

sections.heading: Stats and History
sections.body: Tap [sf:chart.bar] to see total sets, average weight, PRs, and estimated 1RM over time. Tap [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] for a full list of every session that included this exercise.

title: Plan
summary: Schedule workouts, exercises, timers, and voice trainer sessions across your week.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body:
• [sf:list.bullet] — manage plans (rename, set active, edit, delete). Only visible when at least one plan exists.
• [sf:plus] — create a new training plan.

sections.heading: What a Plan Is
sections.body: A Training Plan is a weekly schedule that tells you what to do each day. It can be a repeating weekly template (runs forever) or a fixed-length program (e.g. 8 weeks with different content each week).

sections.heading: Creating a Plan
sections.body:
Tap [sf:plus]. Give it a name and choose:
• Repeating — the same weekly template repeats every week.
• Fixed length — specify a number of weeks; each week can have its own schedule.

Then fill in each day with workouts, exercises, timers, and/or voice trainer configs from your saved lists. Add a note to any day for extra context.

sections.heading: Active Plan
sections.body: One plan is 'active' at a time. The active plan's schedule appears in the Log module's calendar, shows today's tasks at the top, and is referenced by the Plan shortcut tile on the Dashboard.

sections.heading: Manage Plans
sections.body: Tap [sf:list.bullet] to open the manage sheet. From here you can switch which plan is active, edit any plan, or delete plans.

sections.heading: Exporting and Importing Plans
sections.body: Plans can be exported and imported via Settings → Data → Export Plans / Import Plans. The export bundles all referenced workouts, exercises, muscles, equipment, voice trainer configs, and timer configs so the plan is fully portable.

title: Log
summary: Your full workout history — calendar and timeline views, plus journal.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body:
• [sf:magnifyingglass] — search all logged sessions by name or exercise.
• [sf:square.and.pencil] — create a new journal entry for the selected date.
• [sf:calendar.badge.checkmark] — open your Training Plan. Highlights in accent color when a plan is active.
• [sf:chart.bar] / [sf:chart.bar.fill] — toggle the 60-day Activity Ribbon on/off. Blue when the ribbon is showing.
• [sf:list.bullet.below.rectangle] / [sf:calendar] — switch between Timeline view and Calendar view.
• [sf:line.3.horizontal.decrease] — filter what appears on the calendar: Workouts, Journal entries, and/or Plan days.

sections.heading: Calendar Navigation
sections.body:
• [sf:chevron.left] / [sf:chevron.right] — move backward or forward by month (or by week when the calendar is collapsed or in weekly timeline mode).
• Month/week label (center) — tap to jump to any month using the month picker.
• [sf:chevron.down] at the bottom of the single week strip — expand back to the full month view.

sections.heading: Calendar View
sections.body: Days with logged workouts are marked. Tap a date to see what was logged. Plan days are also shown when the Plan filter is on.

sections.heading: Timeline View
sections.body: Sessions as cards in reverse chronological order. Use the Week/Month toggle at the top to scope the list.

sections.heading: Activity Ribbon
sections.body: A 60-day heat map of training frequency. Darker squares mean more activity. Journal entries appear as dots.

sections.heading: Journal
sections.body: Tap [sf:square.and.pencil] to write a free-form note for any date. These appear as dots on the Activity Ribbon and as entries in the log.

title: Trainer
summary: Voice-guided sessions — the app calls out your cues at timed intervals.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body: • [sf:plus] — create a new voice trainer config.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each config row shows buttons:
• [sf:play.circle.fill] — start a session immediately.
• [sf:star] / [sf:star.fill] — toggle favorite (pins to the Favorites grid).
• [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — view session history.
• [sf:chart.bar] — view usage stats.
• [sf:pencil] — edit the config.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited configs appear as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top — tap to launch a session.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens history.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body: Write cues (one per line), set your timing, and the app reads them aloud during your session. Works great for boxing, martial arts, circuits, or any interval training.

sections.heading: Creating a Config
sections.body:
Tap [sf:plus]. Name it, enter cues (e.g. 'Jab', '1-2', 'Right Hook'), then set:
• Rounds and round length
• Rest between rounds
• Delay between spoken cues
• Start warning countdown
• Rest end warning
• Cooldown period

sections.heading: Random Order
sections.body: Shuffles cues each round so sessions stay unpredictable.

sections.heading: Running a Session
sections.body: Tap [sf:play.circle.fill] to start. The screen shows the current cue, round, and elapsed time. Tap Pause to pause. Completing saves the session to the Log.

title: Generate
summary: Auto-generate a workout from your equipment and muscle filters.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body:
• [sf:slider.horizontal.3] — modify saved configurations.
• [sf:star] — save selected configuration for quick generation later.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body: Choose which equipment and muscle groups to include, then tap Generate. The app picks a random set of matching exercises and builds a workout for you.

sections.heading: Saving
sections.body: Tap Save as Workout, give it a name, and it appears in your Workouts list. Or, tap Start Workout to kickoff the session.

sections.heading: Filter Presets
sections.body: Saved filter combinations appear as tiles at the top. Tap one to apply that filter instantly (e.g. 'Home' vs. 'Gym').

title: Timer
summary: Create and run interval timers for any training style.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body: • [sf:plus] — create a new timer config.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each timer row shows buttons:
• [sf:play.circle.fill] — start the timer immediately.
• [sf:star] / [sf:star.fill] — toggle favorite (pins to the Favorites grid).
• [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — view session history for this timer.
• [sf:chart.bar] — view usage stats.
• [sf:pencil] — edit the timer config.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited timers appear as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top — tap to launch the timer.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens history.

sections.heading: Creating a Timer
sections.body: Tap [sf:plus]. Set a name, type, duration, and round count. Optional: warmup, cooldown.

sections.heading: Running
sections.body: Tap [sf:play.circle.fill] to start. The screen shows current phase (work/rest), time remaining, and round. Tap Pause to pause, Resume to continue.

sections.heading: Completing
sections.body: Tap Complete to add the session to Logs.

title: Muscles
summary: Define muscle groups used in exercise tracking and anatomy views.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body: • [sf:plus] — add a new muscle group.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each muscle row shows buttons:
• [sf:chart.bar] — view training frequency and which exercises target this muscle.
• [sf:pencil] — edit the muscle group name or custom image.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited muscles appear as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top [sf:figure] — tap to view detail.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens history.

sections.heading: Adding Muscle Groups
sections.body: Tap [sf:plus]. Name the muscle group. It becomes available to assign to exercises, which powers anatomy diagrams and distribution stats.

sections.heading: Custom Images
sections.body: Each new muscle group created can have custom images assigned.

title: Equipment
summary: Add equipment to categorize exercises and filter generated workouts.

sections.heading: Header Buttons
sections.body: • [sf:plus] — add a new equipment item.

sections.heading: Row Buttons
sections.body:
Each equipment row shows buttons:
• [sf:chart.bar] — view which exercises use this equipment and how often it appears in your logs.
• [sf:pencil] — rename or edit the equipment item.

sections.heading: Favorites Grid
sections.body:
Favorited equipment appears as 3-piece tiles at the top:
• Top — tap to view detail.
• Bottom-left [sf:chart.bar.fill] — opens stats.
• Bottom-right [sf:clock.arrow.circlepath] — opens history.

sections.heading: Adding Equipment
sections.body: Tap [sf:plus]. Name it (e.g. Barbell, Dumbbell, Resistance Band). Assign equipment to exercises in the Exercise editor.

sections.heading: Filtering in Generate
sections.body: In the Generate module, enable the Equipment filter to only include exercises matching your available gear.

title: Mini Games
summary: Fun extras built into the app.

sections.heading: Workout Match
sections.body:
A match-3 puzzle game with 1000 levels.

Swap adjacent tiles to match 3 or more of the same type. Larger matches earn powerups. If you get stuck, a tile will visibly shake as a hint. If you can't beat a level first try, no worries — the next attempt will have more moves available.

Your level saves automatically. Add the Workout Match tile to your Dashboard to see your current level at a glance.

sections.heading: Stick Figure Animator
sections.body:
Build frame-by-frame stick figure animations, export as GIFs, and attach them to exercises as how-to references.

• Frame Editor — drag joints to pose the figure. Use X/Y controls to position it on the canvas. Adjust scale, limb thickness, and body proportions with the sliders below.
• Saving Frames — tap 'Save Frame', give it a name. Open the frame list to load, copy, rename, or delete frames.
• Animation Studio — tap 'Open Studio' to arrange frames into a sequence, set speed, preview, and export as a GIF.
• Adding Objects — tap 'Add Object' to place emojis, images, or colored boxes on the canvas. Each object can be moved, scaled, and rotated independently.
